record.text:
Gaia2: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Dynamic and Asynchronous Environments Article URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11964 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48430918 Points: 2 # Comment...


Origin | Interest | Match

external.description: We introduce Gaia2, a benchmark for evaluating large language model agents in realistic, asynchronous environments. Unlike prior static or synchronous evaluations, Gaia2 introduces scenarios where environments evolve independently of agent actions, requiring agents to operate under temporal constraints, adapt to noisy and dynamic events, resolve ambiguity, and collaborate with other agents. Each scenario is paired with a write-action verifier, enabling fine-grained, action-level evaluation and making Gaia2 directly usable for reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards. Our evaluation of state-of-the-art proprietary and open-source models shows that no model dominates across capabilities: GPT-5 (high) reaches the strongest overall score of 42% pass@1 but fails on time-sensitive tasks, Claude-4 Sonnet trades accuracy and speed for cost, Kimi-K2 leads among open-source models with 21% pass@1. These results highlight fundamental trade-offs between reasoning, efficiency, robustness, and expose challenges in closing the "sim2real" gap. Gaia2 is built on a consumer environment with the open-source Agents Research Environments platform and designed to be easy to extend. By releasing Gaia2 alongside the foundational ARE framework, we aim to provide the community with a flexible infrastructure for developing, benchmarking, and training the next generation of practical agent systems.
external.title: Gaia2: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Dynamic and Asynchronous Environments

The ai.txt and llms.txt angle is underrated. Most SaaS tools we audit have no entity context in crawlable format. The issue is not just being crawlable but being cited as the category authority. What format are you recommending for the structured data layer?

Tracking AI visibility is tricky because attribution lags by days. One thing we found: cross-referencing query answer snapshots weekly gives you a cleaner signal than one-off tests. Are you tracking specific query types (branded vs category) separately?

Start becoming. 📖✨ #Libraries #LibraryStrategy #FuturesThinking #SeriousInsights

external.description: Daniel W. Rasmus on Strategy for Libraries: My 2026 Computers in Libraries Presentation: Strategies for the Win Strategy is supposed to give you confidence about the future, yet most strategic plans are already obsolete by the time the ink dries. In an era when artificial intelligence, geopolitics, and funding realities all shift faster than conference programs can be printed, libraries cannot afford five‑year plans that sit on a shelf. In my 2026 Computers in Libraries session, “Strategies for the Win,” I argued that strategy is not a glossy document or a once‑a‑cycle retreat; it is an evolving story organizations tell themselves about who they are becoming and which obstacles they are willing to name and overcome.
external.title: Daniel W. Rasmus on Strategy for Libraries: My 2026 Computers in Libraries Presentation: Strategies for the Win

Anthropicが評価額9650億ドルでIPOを申請し、GitHub Copilotの新料金には法人利用者の不満が噴出した。

同じ週にSBIホールディングスと日立製作所がAnthropicと相次ぎ手を組んだ。

AI基盤を選ぶ材料が、機能の外側——資本・料金・誠実さ・身元——へ広がり始めている。

機能比較は触って試せば済む。与信審査は相手の経営と料金設計を読む仕事だ。AI選びは後者に近づいている。

今週はほかに6件まとめた。
📝 週報はこちら→
https://note.com/hpn571/n/n947be4419c91

record.text:
配膳ロボット導入5年の結論が出揃った。すかいらーく3,000台、物語コーポレーションは1店舗最大7台、サイゼリヤは全店展開停止→レイアウト変更後に再導入。

明暗を分けたのは機械ではなく「人間の歩行をどれだけ代替できるか」という業態構造だ。注文が頻発する大箱の焼肉は最適、時速5kmで複数皿を運ぶサイゼリヤでは人間が勝つ。

ロボット単体のROIを開示する企業はもうない。投資案件から店舗インフラへ、評価軸が変わった。

6社横断で比較した。考察は記事で。
📝 記事はこちら→ https://note.com/hpn571/n/n76050844afbd

external.description: なぜ今、配膳ロボを横断で読むか  外食チェーンの店内で配膳ロボットを見かけることが、もはや珍しくなくなった。コロナ禍の非接触ニーズと人手不足を背景に、国内チェーンが一斉導入に動いたのが2021年前後。それから約5年が経ち、各社の「結論」が出揃いつつある。  3,000台を一気に展開した会社がある。一度全店展開を止め、店のレイアウトを変えてから入れ直した会社がある。1店舗に最大7台を詰め込み、配膳業務の大半をロボットに任せる会社がある。同じ機械を入れたはずなのに、なぜここまで違う結論になったのか。  本シリーズではこれまで20社の外食上場企業のAI戦略を1社ずつ考察してきたが、今回は初の

value.text:
If you're an academic and are worried about AI and feel powerless, don't let it get to you. It's actually what they want. They want us to burn out and give up.

You can turn off the internet and chill. I promise. All you need to do is stay strong, with breaks of course. From an email I sent an ally:

images.alt:
You all rock. Remind them that when a building is failing all bricks and rebar need to play their role. The mortar cannot worry what the windows are doing — we all need to stay put to keep it standing. Our role at work is to also "stay put" and speak out in the literature and in outreach. We form the protective knowledge core that others will build on. That is why academics paid by the state / with legal protections like tenure etc, exist. To be collectively held to account to produce knowledge to protect from, and inform wider society on, fascism. 😊

Stay strong! Solidarity.

Optimism of the will. Pessimism of the intellect.

1日前・その他・75,000件のポスト

東京都杉並区長選挙（6月28日投票）で、現職の岸本さとこ氏（51）が自民党推薦の大和田伸氏（45、区議）と争う中、大和田氏の YouTube動画がチャンネル登録200人台で50万回再生を記録したことがネット工作疑惑として指摘されている。岸本氏は区立児童相談所開設や生理用品無料化など福祉施策を推進し、自民党区議団の過去の福祉関連議案反対（10件中9件）を批判。一方、大和田氏は15年の区議経験を活かし、現市政の偏りを問題視。岸本支持派はデマに法的対応を宜言し、6月7日に大決起集会を予定している。

record.text:
A genuine problem that a lot of people, this writer included, have is realizing that not every work of art is "for" them. Maybe that movie is in a genre you don't like. Maybe that music gives you a headache. Maybe that game is too hard.

That's okay!
